functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Displays a dialog that lets the user choose a new or existing file on
their filesystem.
functions.syntax: void SDL_ShowSaveFileDialog(SDL_DialogFileCallback callback, void *userdata, SDL_Window *window, const SDL_DialogFileFilter *filters, int nfilters, const char *default_location);

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description:
a function pointer to be invoked when the user selects a file and
accepts, or cancels the dialog, or an error occurs.

parameters.name: userdata
parameters.description:
an optional pointer to pass extra data to the callback when it will
be invoked.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description:
the window that the dialog should be modal for, may be NULL. Not all
platforms support this option.

parameters.name: filters
parameters.description:
a list of filters, may be NULL. Not all platforms support this
option, and platforms that do support it may allow the user to ignore
the filters. If non-NULL, it must remain valid at least until the
callback is invoked.

parameters.name: nfilters
parameters.description: the number of filters. Ignored if filters is NULL.

parameters.name: default_location
parameters.description:
the default folder or file to start the dialog at, may be NULL. Not
all platforms support this option.

remarks:
This is an asynchronous function; it will return immediately, and the
result will be passed to the callback.

The callback will be invoked with a null-terminated list of files the
user chose. The list will be empty if the user canceled the dialog, and
it will be NULL if an error occurred.

Note that the callback may be called from a different thread than the
one the function was invoked on.
The chosen file may or may not already exist.

On Linux, dialogs may require XDG Portals, which requires DBus, which
requires an event-handling loop. Apps that do not use SDL to handle
events should add a call to SDL_PumpEvents in their main loop.
functions.threadSafety:
This function should be called only from the main thread. The
callback may be invoked from the same thread or from a different one,
depending on the OS's constraints.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_ShowSimpleMessageBox
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Display a simple modal message box.
functions.syntax: bool SDL_ShowSimpleMessageBox(SDL_MessageBoxFlags flags, const char *title, const char *message, SDL_Window *window);

parameters.name: flags
parameters.description:
an SDL_MessageBoxFlags
value.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: UTF-8 title text.

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: UTF-8 message text.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: the parent window, or NULL for no parent.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks: If your needs aren't complex, this function is preferred over SDL_ShowMessageBox .
flags may be any of the following:

SDL_MESSAGEBOX_ERROR :
error dialog

SDL_MESSAGEBOX_WARNING :
warning dialog

SDL_MESSAGEBOX_INFORMATION :
informational dialog

This function should be called on the thread that created the parent
window, or on the main thread if the messagebox has no parent. It will
block execution of that thread until the user clicks a button or closes
the messagebox.

This function may be called at any time, even before SDL_Init (). This makes it useful for reporting
errors like a failure to create a renderer or OpenGL context.

On X11, SDL rolls its own dialog box with X11 primitives instead of a
formal toolkit like GTK+ or Qt.

Note that if SDL_Init () would fail
because there isn't any available video target, this function is likely
to fail for the same reasons. If this is a concern, check the return
value from this function and fall back to writing to stderr if you
can.
functions.threadSafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_SignalAsyncIOQueue
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Wake up any threads that are blocking in SDL_WaitAsyncIOResult ().

parameters.name: queue
parameters.description: the async I/O task queue to signal.

remarks:
This will unblock any threads that are sleeping in a call to SDL_WaitAsyncIOResult for the
specified queue, and cause them to return from that function.

This can be useful when destroying a queue to make sure nothing is
touching it indefinitely. In this case, once this call completes, the
caller should take measures to make sure any previously-blocked threads
have returned from their wait and will not touch the queue again
(perhaps by setting a flag to tell the threads to terminate and then
using SDL_WaitThread () to make sure
they've done so).
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_snprintf
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
This works exactly like snprintf() but doesn't require access to a C
runtime.
functions.syntax: int SDL_snprintf(char *text, size_t maxlen, const char *fmt, ...);

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: the buffer to write the string into. Must not be NULL.

parameters.name: maxlen
parameters.description: the maximum bytes to write, including the null-terminator.

parameters.name: fmt
parameters.description: a printf-style format string. Must not be NULL.
a list of values to be used with the format string.

functions.returns:
(int) Returns the number of bytes that should be written, not
counting the null-terminator char, or a negative value on error.
remarks:
Format a string of up to maxlen -1 bytes, converting each
'%' item with values provided through variable arguments.

While some C runtimes differ on how to deal with too-large strings,
this function null-terminates the output, by treating the
null-terminator as part of the maxlen count. Note that if
 maxlen is zero, however, no bytes will be written at
all.

This function returns the number of bytes (not
 characters ) that should be written, excluding the
null-terminator character. If this returns a number >=
 maxlen , it means the output string was truncated. A
negative return value means an error occurred.

Referencing the output string's pointer with a format item is
undefined behavior.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.description: Start accepting Unicode text input events in a window.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: the window to enable text input.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks:
This function will enable text input ( SDL_EVENT_TEXT_INPUT and SDL_EVENT_TEXT_EDITING events) in
the specified window. Please use this function paired with SDL_StopTextInput ().
Text input events are not received by default.

On some platforms using this function shows the screen keyboard
and/or activates an IME, which can prevent some key press events from
being passed through.

functions.name: SDL_StartTextInputWithProperties
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.

Start accepting Unicode text input events in a window, with
properties describing the input.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: the window to enable text input.

parameters.name: props
parameters.description: the properties to use.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks:
This function will enable text input ( SDL_EVENT_TEXT_INPUT and SDL_EVENT_TEXT_EDITING events) in
the specified window. Please use this function paired with SDL_StopTextInput ().
Text input events are not received by default.

On some platforms using this function shows the screen keyboard
and/or activates an IME, which can prevent some key press events from
being passed through.
These are the supported properties:

SDL_PROP_TEXTINPUT_TYPE_NUMBER
- an SDL_TextInputType value that
describes text being input, defaults to SDL_TEXTINPUT_TYPE_TEXT .

SDL_PROP_TEXTINPUT_CAPITALIZATION_NUMBER

 an SDL_Capitalization value
that describes how text should be capitalized, defaults to SDL_CAPITALIZE_SENTENCES for
normal text entry, SDL_CAPITALIZE_WORDS for SDL_TEXTINPUT_TYPE_TEXT_NAME ,
and SDL_CAPITALIZE_NONE for
e-mail addresses, usernames, and passwords.

SDL_PROP_TEXTINPUT_AUTOCORRECT_BOOLEAN

 true to enable auto completion and auto correction, defaults to
true.

SDL_PROP_TEXTINPUT_MULTILINE_BOOLEAN

 true if multiple lines of text are allowed. This defaults to true if
 SDL_HINT_RETURN_KEY_HIDES_IME
is "0" or is not set, and defaults to false if SDL_HINT_RETURN_KEY_HIDES_IME
is "1".
On Android you can directly specify the input type:

SDL_PROP_TEXTINPUT_ANDROID_INPUTTYPE_NUMBER

 the text input type to use, overriding other properties. This is
documented at https://developer.android.com/reference/android/text/InputType

functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_StepBackUTF8
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Decode a UTF-8 string in reverse, one Unicode codepoint at a
time.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: a pointer to the beginning of the UTF-8 string.

parameters.name: pstr
parameters.description: a pointer to a UTF-8 string pointer to be read and adjusted.

functions.returns:
( Uint32 ) Returns the previous Unicode
codepoint in the string.
remarks:
This will go to the start of the previous Unicode codepoint in the
string, move *pstr to that location and return that
codepoint.

If *pstr is already at the start of the string), it will
not advance *pstr at all.

Generally this function is called in a loop until it returns zero,
adjusting its parameter each iteration.
If an invalid UTF-8 sequence is encountered, this function returns SDL_INVALID_UNICODE_CODEPOINT .

Several things can generate invalid UTF-8 sequences, including
overlong encodings, the use of UTF-16 surrogate values, and truncated
data. Please refer to RFC3629 for details.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_StepUTF8
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Decode a UTF-8 string, one Unicode codepoint at a time.

parameters.name: pstr
parameters.description: a pointer to a UTF-8 string pointer to be read and adjusted.

parameters.name: pslen
parameters.description:
a pointer to the number of bytes in the string, to be read and
adjusted. NULL is allowed.

functions.returns:
( Uint32 ) Returns the first Unicode
codepoint in the string.
remarks:
This will return the first Unicode codepoint in the UTF-8 encoded
string in *pstr , and then advance *pstr past
any consumed bytes before returning.

It will not access more than *pslen bytes from the
string. *pslen will be adjusted, as well, subtracting the
number of bytes consumed.

pslen is allowed to be NULL, in which case the string
 must be NULL-terminated, as the function will blindly read
until it sees the NULL char.

if *pslen is zero, it assumes the end of string is
reached and returns a zero codepoint regardless of the contents of the
string buffer.

If the resulting codepoint is zero (a NULL terminator), or
 *pslen is zero, it will not advance *pstr or
 *pslen at all.

Generally this function is called in a loop until it returns zero,
adjusting its parameters each iteration.

If an invalid UTF-8 sequence is encountered, this function returns SDL_INVALID_UNICODE_CODEPOINT
and advances the string/length by one byte (which is to say, a multibyte
sequence might produce several SDL_INVALID_UNICODE_CODEPOINT
returns before it syncs to the next valid UTF-8 sequence).

Several things can generate invalid UTF-8 sequences, including
overlong encodings, the use of UTF-16 surrogate values, and truncated
data. Please refer to RFC3629 for details.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_strcasecmp
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Compare two null-terminated UTF-8 strings, case-insensitively.

parameters.name: str1
parameters.description: the first string to compare. NULL is not permitted!

parameters.name: str2
parameters.description: the second string to compare. NULL is not permitted!

functions.returns:
(int) Returns less than zero if str1 is "less than" str2, greater
than zero if str1 is "greater than" str2, and zero if the strings match
exactly.
remarks:
This will work with Unicode strings, using a technique called
"case-folding" to handle the vast majority of case-sensitive human
languages regardless of system locale. It can deal with expanding
values: a German Eszett character can compare against two ASCII 's'
chars and be considered a match, for example. A notable exception: it
does not handle the Turkish 'i' character; human language is
complicated!

Since this handles Unicode, it expects the string to be well-formed
UTF-8 and not a null-terminated string of arbitrary bytes. Bytes that
are not valid UTF-8 are treated as Unicode character U+FFFD (REPLACEMENT
CHARACTER), which is to say two strings of random bits may turn out to
match if they convert to the same amount of replacement characters.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

 needle in the string, or NULL if not found.
remarks:
This will work with Unicode strings, using a technique called
"case-folding" to handle the vast majority of case-sensitive human
languages regardless of system locale. It can deal with expanding
values: a German Eszett character can compare against two ASCII 's'
chars and be considered a match, for example. A notable exception: it
does not handle the Turkish 'i' character; human language is
complicated!

Since this handles Unicode, it expects the strings to be well-formed
UTF-8 and not a null-terminated string of arbitrary bytes. Bytes that
are not valid UTF-8 are treated as Unicode character U+FFFD (REPLACEMENT
CHARACTER), which is to say two strings of random bits may turn out to
match if they convert to the same amount of replacement characters.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.

parameters.name: str2
parameters.description: the second string to compare. NULL is not permitted!

functions.returns:
(int) Returns less than zero if str1 is "less than" str2, greater
than zero if str1 is "greater than" str2, and zero if the strings match
exactly.

functions.description: Concatenate strings.
functions.syntax: size_t SDL_strlcat(char *dst, const char *src, size_t maxlen);

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description:
The destination buffer already containing the first null-terminated
string. Must not be NULL and must not overlap with
 src .

parameters.name: src
parameters.description:
The second null-terminated string. Must not be NULL, and must not
overlap with dst .

parameters.name: maxlen
parameters.description: The length (in characters) of the destination buffer.

functions.returns:
(size_t) Returns the length (in characters, excluding the null
terminator) of the string in dst plus the length of
 src .
remarks:
This function appends up to maxlen - SDL_strlen (dst) - 1 characters from
 src to the end of the string in dst , then
appends a null terminator.
src and dst must not overlap.

If maxlen - SDL_strlen (dst) - 1 is less than or equal to
0, then dst is unmodified.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.description: Copy a string.
functions.syntax: size_t SDL_strlcpy(char *dst, const char *src, size_t maxlen);

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description:
The destination buffer. Must not be NULL, and must not overlap with
 src .

parameters.name: src
parameters.description:
The null-terminated string to copy. Must not be NULL, and must not
overlap with dst .

parameters.name: maxlen
parameters.description: The length (in characters) of the destination buffer.

functions.returns:
(size_t) Returns the length (in characters, excluding the null
terminator) of src .
remarks:
This function copies up to maxlen - 1 characters from
 src to dst , then appends a null
terminator.

If maxlen is 0, no characters are copied and no null
terminator is written.

If you want to copy an UTF-8 string but need to ensure that
multi-byte sequences are not truncated, consider using SDL_utf8strlcpy ().
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

convert ASCII values 'A' through 'Z' to lowercase.

This function operates on a null-terminated string of bytes--even if
it is malformed UTF-8!--and converts ASCII characters 'A' through 'Z' to
their lowercase equivalents in-place, returning the original
 str pointer.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_strncasecmp
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Compare two UTF-8 strings, case-insensitively, up to a number of
bytes.
functions.syntax: int SDL_strncasecmp(const char *str1, const char *str2, size_t maxlen);

parameters.name: str1
parameters.description: the first string to compare. NULL is not permitted!

parameters.name: str2
parameters.description: the second string to compare. NULL is not permitted!

parameters.name: maxlen
parameters.description: the maximum number of bytes to compare.

functions.returns:
(int) Returns less than zero if str1 is "less than" str2, greater
than zero if str1 is "greater than" str2, and zero if the strings match
exactly.
remarks:
This will work with Unicode strings, using a technique called
"case-folding" to handle the vast majority of case-sensitive human
languages regardless of system locale. It can deal with expanding
values: a German Eszett character can compare against two ASCII 's'
chars and be considered a match, for example. A notable exception: it
does not handle the Turkish 'i' character; human language is
complicated!

Since this handles Unicode, it expects the string to be well-formed
UTF-8 and not a null-terminated string of arbitrary bytes. Bytes that
are not valid UTF-8 are treated as Unicode character U+FFFD (REPLACEMENT
CHARACTER), which is to say two strings of random bits may turn out to
match if they convert to the same amount of replacement characters.

Note that while this function is intended to be used with UTF-8,
 maxlen specifies a byte limit! If the limit lands
in the middle of a multi-byte UTF-8 sequence, it may convert a portion
of the final character to one or more Unicode character U+FFFD
(REPLACEMENT CHARACTER) so as not to overflow a buffer.

maxlen specifies a maximum number of bytes to compare;
if the strings match to this number of bytes (or both have matched to a
null-terminator character before this number of bytes), they will be
considered equal.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_strncmp
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Compare two UTF-8 strings up to a number of bytes.
functions.syntax: int SDL_strncmp(const char *str1, const char *str2, size_t maxlen);

parameters.name: str1
parameters.description: the first string to compare. NULL is not permitted!

parameters.name: str2
parameters.description: the second string to compare. NULL is not permitted!

parameters.name: maxlen
parameters.description: the maximum number of bytes to compare.

functions.returns:
(int) Returns less than zero if str1 is "less than" str2, greater
than zero if str1 is "greater than" str2, and zero if the strings match
exactly.
remarks:
Due to the nature of UTF-8 encoding, this will work with Unicode
strings, since effectively this function just compares bytes until it
hits a null-terminating character. Also due to the nature of UTF-8, this
can be used with SDL_qsort () to put strings
in (roughly) alphabetical order.

Note that while this function is intended to be used with UTF-8, it
is doing a bytewise comparison, and maxlen specifies a
 byte limit! If the limit lands in the middle of a multi-byte
UTF-8 sequence, it will only compare a portion of the final
character.

maxlen specifies a maximum number of bytes to compare;
if the strings match to this number of bytes (or both have matched to a
null-terminator character before this number of bytes), they will be
considered equal.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

remarks:

 str , up to maxlen bytes, using SDL_malloc , and then makes a copy of the
string into this space.

If the string is longer than maxlen bytes, the returned
string will be maxlen bytes long, plus a null-terminator
character that isn't included in the count.

The returned string is owned by the caller, and should be passed to
 SDL_free when no longer needed.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.description:

remarks:

If you need the length of a UTF-8 string, consider using SDL_utf8strnlen ().
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_strnstr
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Search a string, up to n bytes, for the first instance of a specific
substring.
functions.syntax: char * SDL_strnstr(const char *haystack, const char *needle, size_t maxlen);

parameters.name: haystack
parameters.description: the string to search. Must not be NULL.

parameters.name: needle
parameters.description: the string to search for. Must not be NULL.

parameters.name: maxlen
parameters.description: the maximum number of bytes to search in haystack .

functions.returns:
(char *) Returns a pointer to the first instance of
 needle in the string, or NULL if not found.
remarks:
The search ends once it finds the requested substring, or a null
terminator byte to end the string, or maxlen bytes have

 characters , so it's legal to search for malformed and

functions.description: Search a string for the last instance of a specific byte.

functions.description: Search a string for the first instance of a specific substring.

parameters.name: haystack
parameters.description: the string to search. Must not be NULL.

parameters.name: needle
parameters.description: the string to search for. Must not be NULL.

functions.returns:
(char *) Returns a pointer to the first instance of
 needle in the string, or NULL if not found.
remarks:
The search ends once it finds the requested substring, or a null
terminator byte to end the string.

Note that this looks for strings of bytes , not

functions.name: SDL_strtod

functions.description: Parse a double from a string.

parameters.name: str
parameters.description: the null-terminated string to read. Must not be NULL.

parameters.name: endp
parameters.description:
if not NULL, the address of the first invalid character (i.e. the
next character after the parsed number) will be written to this
pointer.

functions.returns:
(double) Returns the parsed double , or 0 if no number
could be parsed.
remarks:
This function makes fewer guarantees than the C runtime
 strtod :

Only decimal notation is guaranteed to be supported. The handling of
scientific and hexadecimal notation is unspecified.
Whether or not INF and NAN can be parsed is unspecified.
The precision of the result is unspecified.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.

functions.name: SDL_strtok_r
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
This works exactly like strtok_r() but doesn't require access to a C
runtime.

parameters.name: str
parameters.description: the string to tokenize, or NULL to continue tokenizing.

parameters.name: delim
parameters.description: the delimiter string that separates tokens.

parameters.name: saveptr
parameters.description: pointer to a char *, used for ongoing state.

functions.returns:
(char *) Returns A pointer to the next token, or NULL if no tokens
remain.
remarks: Break a string up into a series of tokens.

To start tokenizing a new string, str should be the
non-NULL address of the string to start tokenizing. Future calls to get
the next token from the same string should specify a NULL.

Note that this function will overwrite pieces of str
with null chars to split it into tokens. This function cannot be used
with const/read-only strings!

saveptr just needs to point to a char *
that can be overwritten; SDL will use this to save tokenizing state
between calls. It is initialized if str is non-NULL, and

functions.description: Parse a long from a string.
functions.syntax: long SDL_strtol(const char *str, char **endp, int base);

parameters.name: str
parameters.description: The null-terminated string to read. Must not be NULL.

parameters.name: endp
parameters.description:
If not NULL, the address of the first invalid character (i.e. the
next character after the parsed number) will be written to this
pointer.

parameters.name: base
parameters.description:
The base of the integer to read. Supported values are 0 and 2 to 36
inclusive. If 0, the base will be inferred from the number's prefix (0x
for hexadecimal, 0 for octal, decimal otherwise).

functions.returns:
(long) Returns the parsed long , or 0 if no number could
be parsed.
remarks:
If str starts with whitespace, then those whitespace
characters are skipped before attempting to parse the number.

If the parsed number does not fit inside a long , the
result is clamped to the minimum and maximum representable
 long values.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

Submits a command buffer so its commands can be processed on the
GPU.

parameters.name: command_buffer
parameters.description: a command buffer.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success, false on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks: It is invalid to use the command buffer after this is called.

This must be called from the thread the command buffer was acquired
on.

All commands in the submission are guaranteed to begin executing
before any command in a subsequent submission begins executing.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

parameters.description: a command buffer.

associated with the command buffer, or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks:
You must release this fence when it is no longer needed or it will
cause a leak. It is invalid to use the command buffer after this is
called.

This must be called from the thread the command buffer was acquired
on.

All commands in the submission are guaranteed to begin executing
before any command in a subsequent submission begins executing.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.description: Byte-swap an unsigned 16-bit number.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: the value to byte-swap.

functions.returns:
( Uint16 ) Returns x , with its
bytes in the opposite endian order.
remarks:
This will always byte-swap the value, whether it's currently in the
native byteorder of the system or not. You should use SDL_Swap16LE or SDL_Swap16BE instead, in most cases.

Note that this is a forced-inline function in a header, and not a
public API function available in the SDL library (which is to say, the
code is embedded in the calling program and the linker and dynamic
loader will not be able to find this function inside SDL itself).
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_swprintf
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
This works exactly like swprintf() but doesn't require access to a C
runtime.
functions.syntax: int SDL_swprintf(wchar_t *text, size_t maxlen, const wchar_t *fmt, ...);

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: the buffer to write the wide string into. Must not be NULL.

parameters.name: maxlen
parameters.description:
the maximum wchar_t values to write, including the
null-terminator.

parameters.name: fmt
parameters.description: a printf-style format string. Must not be NULL.
a list of values to be used with the format string.

functions.returns:
(int) Returns the number of wide characters that should be written,
not counting the null-terminator char, or a negative value on error.
remarks:
Format a wide string of up to maxlen -1 wchar_t values,
converting each '%' item with values provided through variable
arguments.

While some C runtimes differ on how to deal with too-large strings,
this function null-terminates the output, by treating the
null-terminator as part of the maxlen count. Note that if
 maxlen is zero, however, no wide characters will be written
at all.

This function returns the number of wide characters (not
 codepoints ) that should be written, excluding the
null-terminator character. If this returns a number >=
 maxlen , it means the output string was truncated. A
negative return value means an error occurred.

Referencing the output string's pointer with a format item is
undefined behavior.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_SyncWindow
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Block until any pending window state is finalized.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description:
the window for which to wait for the pending state to be
applied.

functions.returns:
(bool) Returns true on success or false if the operation timed out
before the window was in the requested state.
remarks:
On asynchronous windowing systems, this acts as a synchronization
barrier for pending window state. It will attempt to wait until any
pending window state has been applied and is guaranteed to return within
finite time. Note that for how long it can potentially block depends on
the underlying window system, as window state changes may involve
somewhat lengthy animations that must complete before the window is in
its final requested state.

On windowing systems where changes are immediate, this does
nothing.
functions.threadSafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: floating point value, in radians.

functions.returns: (double) Returns tangent of x .
remarks: Domain: -INF <= x <= INF
Range: -INF <= y <= INF

This function operates on double-precision floating point values, use
 SDL_tanf for single-precision floats.

This function may use a different approximation across different
versions, platforms and configurations. i.e, it can return a different
value given the same input on different machines or operating systems,
or if SDL is updated.

functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Locking for atomic access to the joystick API.
functions.returns:
(bool) Returns true if the joysticks were successfully locked, false
otherwise.
remarks:
The SDL joystick functions are thread-safe, however you can lock the
joysticks while processing to guarantee that the joystick list won't
change and joystick and gamepad events will not be delivered.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.6.0.

functions.name: SDL_TryLockMutex
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Try to lock a mutex without blocking.

parameters.name: mutex
parameters.description: the mutex to try to lock.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success, false if the mutex would block.
remarks:
This works just like SDL_LockMutex (), but if the mutex is not
available, this function returns false immediately.

This technique is useful if you need exclusive access to a resource
but don't want to wait for it, and will return to it to try again
later.
This function returns true if passed a NULL mutex.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_TryLockRWLockForReading
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Try to lock a read/write lock for reading without
blocking.

parameters.name: rwlock
parameters.description: the rwlock to try to lock.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success, false if the lock would block.
remarks:
This works just like SDL_LockRWLockForReading (), but
if the rwlock is not available, then this function returns false
immediately.

This technique is useful if you need access to a resource but don't
want to wait for it, and will return to it to try again later.

Trying to lock for read-only access can succeed if other threads are
holding read-only locks, as this won't prevent access.
This function returns true if passed a NULL rwlock.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_TryLockRWLockForWriting
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Try to lock a read/write lock for writing without
blocking.

parameters.name: rwlock
parameters.description: the rwlock to try to lock.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success, false if the lock would block.
remarks:
This works just like SDL_LockRWLockForWriting (), but
if the rwlock is not available, then this function returns false
immediately.

This technique is useful if you need exclusive access to a resource
but don't want to wait for it, and will return to it to try again
later.

It is illegal for the owning thread to lock an already-locked rwlock
for writing (read-only may be locked recursively, writing can not).
Doing so results in undefined behavior.

It is illegal to request a write lock from a thread that already
holds a read-only lock. Doing so results in undefined behavior. Unlock
the read-only lock before requesting a write lock.
This function returns true if passed a NULL rwlock.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_TryLockSpinlock
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Try to lock a spin lock by setting it to a non-zero value.

parameters.name: lock
parameters.description: a pointer to a lock variable.

functions.returns:
(bool) Returns true if the lock succeeded, false if the lock is
already held.
remarks:
Please note that spinlocks are dangerous if you don't
know what you're doing. Please be careful using any sort of
spinlock!
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_TryWaitSemaphore
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
See if a semaphore has a positive value and decrement it if it
does.

parameters.name: sem
parameters.description: the semaphore to wait on.

functions.returns:
(bool) Returns true if the wait succeeds, false if the wait would
block.
remarks:
This function checks to see if the semaphore pointed to by
 sem has a positive value and atomically decrements the
semaphore value if it does. If the semaphore doesn't have a positive
value, the function immediately returns false.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_UCS4ToUTF8
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Convert a single Unicode codepoint to UTF-8.

parameters.name: codepoint
parameters.description: a Unicode codepoint to convert to UTF-8.

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description:
the location to write the encoded UTF-8. Must point to at least 4
bytes!

functions.returns:
(char *) Returns the first byte past the newly-written UTF-8
sequence.
remarks:
The buffer pointed to by dst must be at least 4 bytes
long, as this function may generate between 1 and 4 bytes of output.

This function returns the first byte after the newly-written
UTF-8 sequence, which is useful for encoding multiple codepoints in a
loop, or knowing where to write a NULL-terminator character to end the
string (in either case, plan to have a buffer of more than 4
bytes!).

If codepoint is an invalid value (outside the Unicode
range, or a UTF-16 surrogate value, etc), this will use U+FFFD
(REPLACEMENT CHARACTER) for the codepoint instead, and not set an
error.

If dst is NULL, this returns NULL immediately without
writing to the pointer and without setting an error.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_uitoa
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Convert an unsigned integer into a string.
functions.syntax: char * SDL_uitoa(unsigned int value, char *str, int radix);

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: the unsigned integer to convert.

parameters.name: str
parameters.description: the buffer to write the string into.

parameters.name: radix
parameters.description: the radix to use for string generation.

functions.returns: (char *) Returns str .
remarks:
This requires a radix to specified for string format. Specifying 10
produces a decimal number, 16 hexadecimal, etc. Must be in the range of
2 to 36.

Note that this function will overflow a buffer if str is
not large enough to hold the output! It may be safer to use SDL_snprintf to clamp output, or SDL_asprintf to allocate a buffer.
Otherwise, it doesn't hurt to allocate much more space than you expect
to use (and don't forget null terminator bytes, etc).
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

Otherwise, it doesn't hurt to allocate much more space than you expect

parameters.name: str

functions.returns: (char *) Returns str .

This requires a radix to specified for string format. Specifying 10
produces a decimal number, 16 hexadecimal, etc. Must be in the range of
2 to 36.

Note that this function will overflow a buffer if str is
not large enough to hold the output! It may be safer to use SDL_snprintf to clamp output, or SDL_asprintf to allocate a buffer.
Otherwise, it doesn't hurt to allocate much more space than you expect

functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.

parameters.description: an audio stream to unbind from a device. Can be NULL.

remarks:
This is a convenience function, equivalent to calling
 SDL_UnbindAudioStreams(&stream, 1) .
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.

functions.name: SDL_UnbindAudioStreams
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Unbind a list of audio streams from their audio devices.

parameters.name: streams
parameters.description:
an array of audio streams to unbind. Can be NULL or contain
NULL.

parameters.name: num_streams
parameters.description: number streams listed in the streams array.

remarks:
The streams being unbound do not all have to be on the same device.
All streams on the same device will be unbound atomically (data will
stop flowing through all unbound streams on the same device at the same
time).
Unbinding a stream that isn't bound to a device is a legal no-op.

functions.name: SDL_UnlockMutex

functions.description: Unlock the mutex.

parameters.name: mutex
parameters.description: the mutex to unlock.

remarks:
It is legal for the owning thread to lock an already-locked mutex. It
must unlock it the same number of times before it is actually made
available for other threads in the system (this is known as a "recursive
mutex").

It is illegal to unlock a mutex that has not been locked by the
current thread, and doing so results in undefined behavior.
functions.threadSafety:
This call must be paired with a previous locking call on the same
thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_UnlockRWLock
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Unlock the read/write lock.

parameters.name: rwlock
parameters.description: the rwlock to unlock.

remarks:
Use this function to unlock the rwlock, whether it was locked for
read-only or write operations.

It is legal for the owning thread to lock an already-locked read-only
lock. It must unlock it the same number of times before it is actually
made available for other threads in the system (this is known as a
"recursive rwlock").

It is illegal to unlock a rwlock that has not been locked by the
current thread, and doing so results in undefined behavior.
functions.threadSafety:
This call must be paired with a previous locking call on the same
thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_UnlockSpinlock
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Unlock a spin lock by setting it to 0.

parameters.name: lock
parameters.description: a pointer to a lock variable.

remarks: Always returns immediately.

Please note that spinlocks are dangerous if you don't
know what you're doing. Please be careful using any sort of
spinlock!
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_UnlockTexture

functions.description:
Unlock a texture, uploading the changes to video memory, if
needed.

parameters.name: texture
parameters.description: a texture locked by SDL_LockTexture ().

remarks:
Warning : Please note that SDL_LockTexture () is intended to be
write-only; it will not guarantee the previous contents of the texture
will be provided. You must fully initialize any area of a texture that
you lock before unlocking it, as the pixels might otherwise be
uninitialized memory.

Which is to say: locking and immediately unlocking a texture can
result in corrupted textures, depending on the renderer in use.
functions.threadSafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.description: Deregister the win32 window class from an SDL_RegisterApp call.
remarks: This can be called to undo the effects of SDL_RegisterApp .

Most applications do not need to, and should not, call this directly;
SDL will call it when deinitializing the video subsystem.

It is safe to call this multiple times, as long as every call is
eventually paired with a prior call to SDL_RegisterApp . The window class will
only be deregistered when the registration counter in SDL_RegisterApp decrements to zero
through calls to this function.
functions.threadSafety: This function is not thread safe.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

Update a rectangle within a planar NV12 or NV21 texture with new
pixels.

parameters.name: texture
parameters.description: the texture to update.

parameters.name: rect
parameters.description:
a pointer to the rectangle of pixels to update, or NULL to update
the entire texture.

parameters.name: Yplane

the number of bytes between rows of pixel data for the UV
plane.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks:
You can use SDL_UpdateTexture ()
as long as your pixel data is a contiguous block of NV12/21 planes in
the proper order, but this function is available if your pixel data is

functions.name: SDL_UpdateTexture
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Update the given texture rectangle with new pixel data.
functions.syntax: bool SDL_UpdateTexture(SDL_Texture *texture, const SDL_Rect *rect, const void *pixels, int pitch);

parameters.name: texture
parameters.description: the texture to update.

parameters.name: rect
parameters.description:
an SDL_Rect structure representing the
area to update, or NULL to update the entire texture.

parameters.name: pixels
parameters.description: the raw pixel data in the format of the texture.

parameters.name: pitch
parameters.description:
the number of bytes in a row of pixel data, including padding
between lines.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks:
The pixel data must be in the pixel format of the texture, which can
be queried using the SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_FORMAT_NUMBER
property.

This is a fairly slow function, intended for use with static textures
that do not change often.

If the texture is intended to be updated often, it is preferred to
create the texture as streaming and use the locking functions referenced
below. While this function will work with streaming textures, for
optimization reasons you may not get the pixels back if you lock the
texture afterward.
functions.threadSafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_UpdateTrays
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Update the trays.
remarks:
This is called automatically by the event loop and is only needed if
you're using trays but aren't handling SDL events.
functions.threadSafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_UpdateWindowSurface
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Copy the window surface to the screen.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: the window to update.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks:
This is the function you use to reflect any changes to the surface on
the screen.
This function is equivalent to the SDL 1.2 API SDL_Flip ().
functions.threadSafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_UpdateWindowSurfaceRects
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Copy areas of the window surface to the screen.
functions.syntax: bool SDL_UpdateWindowSurfaceRects(SDL_Window *window, const SDL_Rect *rects, int numrects);

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: the window to update.

parameters.name: rects
parameters.description:
an array of SDL_Rect structures
representing areas of the surface to copy, in pixels.

parameters.name: numrects
parameters.description: the number of rectangles.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks:
This is the function you use to reflect changes to portions of the
surface on the screen.
This function is equivalent to the SDL 1.2 API SDL_UpdateRects ().

Note that this function will update at least the rectangles
specified, but this is only intended as an optimization; in practice,
this might update more of the screen (or all of the screen!), depending
on what method SDL uses to send pixels to the system.
functions.threadSafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_UpdateYUVTexture
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Update a rectangle within a planar YV12 or IYUV texture with new
pixel data.

parameters.name: texture
parameters.description: the texture to update.

parameters.name: rect
parameters.description:
a pointer to the rectangle of pixels to update, or NULL to update
the entire texture.

parameters.name: Yplane
parameters.description: the raw pixel data for the Y plane.

parameters.name: Ypitch
parameters.description: the number of bytes between rows of pixel data for the Y plane.

parameters.name: Uplane
parameters.description: the raw pixel data for the U plane.

parameters.name: Upitch
parameters.description: the number of bytes between rows of pixel data for the U plane.

parameters.name: Vplane
parameters.description: the raw pixel data for the V plane.

parameters.name: Vpitch
parameters.description: the number of bytes between rows of pixel data for the V plane.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks:
You can use SDL_UpdateTexture ()
as long as your pixel data is a contiguous block of Y and U/V planes in
the proper order, but this function is available if your pixel data is
not contiguous.
functions.threadSafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_UploadToGPUBuffer
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Uploads data from a transfer buffer to a buffer.

parameters.name: copy_pass
parameters.description: a copy pass handle.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: the source transfer buffer with offset.

parameters.name: destination
parameters.description: the destination buffer with offset and size.

parameters.name: cycle
parameters.description:
if true, cycles the buffer if it is already bound, otherwise
overwrites the data.

remarks:
The upload occurs on the GPU timeline. You may assume that the upload
has finished in subsequent commands.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_UploadToGPUTexture
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Uploads data from a transfer buffer to a texture.

parameters.name: copy_pass
parameters.description: a copy pass handle.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: the source transfer buffer with image layout information.

parameters.name: destination
parameters.description: the destination texture region.

parameters.name: cycle
parameters.description:
if true, cycles the texture if the texture is bound, otherwise
overwrites the data.

remarks:
The upload occurs on the GPU timeline. You may assume that the upload
has finished in subsequent commands.

You must align the data in the transfer buffer to a multiple of the
texel size of the texture format.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_utf8strlcpy
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Copy an UTF-8 string.
functions.syntax: size_t SDL_utf8strlcpy(char *dst, const char *src, size_t dst_bytes);

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description:
The destination buffer. Must not be NULL, and must not overlap with
 src .

parameters.name: src
parameters.description:
The null-terminated UTF-8 string to copy. Must not be NULL, and must
not overlap with dst .

parameters.name: dst_bytes
parameters.description: The length (in bytes) of the destination buffer. Must not be 0.

functions.returns:
(size_t) Returns the number of bytes written, excluding the null
terminator.
remarks:
This function copies up to dst_bytes - 1 bytes from
 src to dst while also ensuring that the string
written to dst does not end in a truncated multi-byte
sequence. Finally, it appends a null terminator.
src and dst must not overlap.

Note that unlike SDL_strlcpy (), this
function returns the number of bytes written, not the length of
 src .
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_utf8strlen
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Count the number of codepoints in a UTF-8 string.

parameters.name: str
parameters.description: The null-terminated UTF-8 string to read. Must not be NULL.

functions.returns:
(size_t) Returns The length (in codepoints, excluding the null
terminator) of src .
remarks:
Counts the codepoints , not bytes , in
 str , excluding the null terminator.
If you need to count the bytes in a string instead, consider using SDL_strlen ().

Since this handles Unicode, it expects the strings to be well-formed
UTF-8 and not a null-terminated string of arbitrary bytes. Bytes that
are not valid UTF-8 are treated as Unicode character U+FFFD (REPLACEMENT
CHARACTER), so a malformed or incomplete UTF-8 sequence might increase
the count by several replacement characters.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_utf8strnlen
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Count the number of codepoints in a UTF-8 string, up to n bytes.

parameters.name: str
parameters.description: The null-terminated UTF-8 string to read. Must not be NULL.

parameters.name: bytes
parameters.description: The maximum amount of bytes to count.

functions.returns:
(size_t) Returns The length (in codepoints, excluding the null
terminator) of src but never more than
 maxlen .
remarks:
Counts the codepoints , not bytes , in
 str , excluding the null terminator.
If you need to count the bytes in a string instead, consider using SDL_strnlen ().

The counting stops at bytes bytes (not codepoints!).
This seems counterintuitive, but makes it easy to express the total size
of the string's buffer.

Since this handles Unicode, it expects the strings to be well-formed
UTF-8 and not a null-terminated string of arbitrary bytes. Bytes that
are not valid UTF-8 are treated as Unicode character U+FFFD (REPLACEMENT
CHARACTER), so a malformed or incomplete UTF-8 sequence might increase
the count by several replacement characters.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_vasprintf
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
This works exactly like vasprintf() but doesn't require access to a C
runtime.
functions.syntax: int SDL_vasprintf(char **strp, const char *fmt, va_list ap);

parameters.name: strp
parameters.description: on output, is set to the new string. Must not be NULL.

parameters.name: fmt
parameters.description: a printf-style format string. Must not be NULL.

parameters.name: ap
parameters.description:
a va_list values to be used with the format
string.

functions.returns:
(int) Returns the number of bytes in the newly-allocated string, not
counting the null-terminator char, or a negative value on error.
remarks:
Functions identically to SDL_asprintf (), except it takes a
 va_list instead of using ... variable
arguments.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

parameters.name: text

parameters.name: maxlen
parameters.description: the maximum bytes to write, including the null-terminator.

parameters.name: fmt
parameters.description: a printf-style format string. Must not be NULL.

parameters.name: ap
parameters.description:
a va_list values to be used with the format
string.

functions.returns:
(int) Returns the number of bytes that should be written, not
counting the null-terminator char, or a negative value on error.
remarks:
Functions identically to SDL_snprintf (), except it takes a
 va_list instead of using ... variable
arguments.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_vsscanf

functions.description:
This works exactly like vsscanf() but doesn't require access to a C

functions.syntax: int SDL_vsscanf(const char *text, const char *fmt, va_list ap);

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: the string to scan. Must not be NULL.

parameters.name: fmt
parameters.description: a printf-style format string. Must not be NULL.

parameters.name: ap
parameters.description:
a va_list of pointers to values to be filled in with
scanned items.

functions.returns: (int) Returns the number of items that matched the format string.
remarks:
Functions identically to SDL_sscanf (),
except it takes a va_list instead of using ...
variable arguments.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.

functions.name: SDL_vswprintf
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.

This works exactly like vswprintf() but doesn't require access to a C

functions.syntax: int SDL_vswprintf(wchar_t *text, size_t maxlen, const wchar_t *fmt, va_list ap);

parameters.name: text

the maximum wide characters to write, including the
null-terminator.

parameters.name: fmt
