functions.name: SDL_Vulkan_CreateSurface
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Create a Vulkan rendering surface for a window.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: the window to which to attach the Vulkan surface.

parameters.name: instance
parameters.description: the Vulkan instance handle.

parameters.name: allocator
parameters.description:
a VkAllocationCallbacks struct, which lets the app set the allocator
that creates the surface. Can be NULL.

parameters.name: surface
parameters.description:
a pointer to a VkSurfaceKHR handle to output the newly created
surface.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks:
The window must have been created with the SDL_WINDOW_VULKAN flag
and instance must have been created with extensions
returned by SDL_Vulkan_GetInstanceExtensions ()
enabled.

If allocator is NULL, Vulkan will use the system default
allocator. This argument is passed directly to Vulkan and isn't used by
SDL itself.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_Vulkan_DestroySurface
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Destroy the Vulkan rendering surface of a window.

parameters.name: instance
parameters.description: the Vulkan instance handle.

parameters.name: surface
parameters.description: vkSurfaceKHR handle to destroy.

parameters.name: allocator
parameters.description:
a VkAllocationCallbacks struct, which lets the app set the allocator
that destroys the surface. Can be NULL.

remarks:
This should be called before SDL_DestroyWindow , if SDL_Vulkan_CreateSurface was
called after SDL_CreateWindow .

The instance must have been created with extensions
returned by SDL_Vulkan_GetInstanceExtensions ()
enabled and surface must have been created successfully by
an SDL_Vulkan_CreateSurface ()
call.

If allocator is NULL, Vulkan will use the system default
allocator. This argument is passed directly to Vulkan and isn't used by
SDL itself.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_Vulkan_GetInstanceExtensions
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Get the Vulkan instance extensions needed for vkCreateInstance.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: a pointer filled in with the number of extensions returned.

functions.returns:
(char const * const *) Returns an array of extension name strings on
success, NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks:
This should be called after either calling SDL_Vulkan_LoadLibrary () or
creating an SDL_Window with the SDL_WINDOW_VULKAN
flag.

On return, the variable pointed to by count will be set
to the number of elements returned, suitable for using with
VkInstanceCreateInfo::enabledExtensionCount, and the returned array can
be used with VkInstanceCreateInfo::ppEnabledExtensionNames, for calling
Vulkan's vkCreateInstance API.
You should not free the returned array; it is owned by SDL.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_Vulkan_GetPresentationSupport
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Query support for presentation via a given physical device and queue
family.

parameters.name: instance
parameters.description: the Vulkan instance handle.

parameters.name: physicalDevice
parameters.description: a valid Vulkan physical device handle.

parameters.name: queueFamilyIndex
parameters.description: a valid queue family index for the given physical device.

functions.returns:
(bool) Returns true if supported, false if unsupported or an error
occurred.
remarks:
The instance must have been created with extensions
returned by SDL_Vulkan_GetInstanceExtensions ()
enabled.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_Vulkan_GetVkGetInstanceProcAddr
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Get the address of the vkGetInstanceProcAddr
function.
functions.returns:
( SDL_FunctionPointer ) Returns
the function pointer for vkGetInstanceProcAddr or NULL on
failure; call SDL_GetError () for more
information.
remarks:
This should be called after either calling SDL_Vulkan_LoadLibrary () or
creating an SDL_Window with the SDL_WINDOW_VULKAN
flag.

The actual type of the returned function pointer is
PFN_vkGetInstanceProcAddr, but that isn't available because the Vulkan
headers are not included here. You should cast the return value of this
function to that type, e.g.
vkGetInstanceProcAddr = (PFN_vkGetInstanceProcAddr)SDL_Vulkan_GetVkGetInstanceProcAddr();
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_Vulkan_LoadLibrary
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Dynamically load the Vulkan loader library.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: the platform dependent Vulkan loader library name or NULL.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks:
This should be called after initializing the video driver, but before
creating any Vulkan windows. If no Vulkan loader library is loaded, the
default library will be loaded upon creation of the first Vulkan
window.

SDL keeps a counter of how many times this function has been
successfully called, so it is safe to call this function multiple times,
so long as it is eventually paired with an equivalent number of calls to
 SDL_Vulkan_UnloadLibrary .
The path argument is ignored unless there is no library
currently loaded, and and the library isn't actually unloaded until
there have been an equivalent number of calls to SDL_Vulkan_UnloadLibrary .

It is fairly common for Vulkan applications to link with libvulkan
instead of explicitly loading it at run time. This will work with SDL
provided the application links to a dynamic library and both it and SDL
use the same search path.

If you specify a non-NULL path , an application should
retrieve all of the Vulkan functions it uses from the dynamic library
using SDL_Vulkan_GetVkGetInstanceProcAddr
unless you can guarantee path points to the same vulkan
loader library the application linked to.

On Apple devices, if path is NULL, SDL will attempt to
find the vkGetInstanceProcAddr address within all the
Mach-O images of the current process. This is because it is fairly
common for Vulkan applications to link with libvulkan (and historically
MoltenVK was provided as a static library). If it is not found, on
macOS, SDL will attempt to load vulkan.framework/vulkan ,
 libvulkan.1.dylib ,
 MoltenVK.framework/MoltenVK , and
 libMoltenVK.dylib , in that order. On iOS, SDL will attempt
to load libMoltenVK.dylib . Applications using a dynamic
framework or .dylib must ensure it is included in its application
bundle.

On non-Apple devices, application linking with a static libvulkan is
not supported. Either do not link to the Vulkan loader or link to a
dynamic library version.
functions.threadSafety: This function is not thread safe.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_Vulkan_UnloadLibrary
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Unload the Vulkan library previously loaded by SDL_Vulkan_LoadLibrary ().
remarks:
SDL keeps a counter of how many times this function has been called,
so it is safe to call this function multiple times, so long as it is
paired with an equivalent number of calls to SDL_Vulkan_LoadLibrary . The
library isn't actually unloaded until there have been an equivalent
number of calls to SDL_Vulkan_UnloadLibrary .

Once the library has actually been unloaded, if any Vulkan instances
remain, they will likely crash the program. Clean up any existing Vulkan
resources, and destroy appropriate windows, renderers and GPU devices
before calling this function.
functions.threadSafety: This function is not thread safe.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WaitAndAcquireGPUSwapchainTexture
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Blocks the thread until a swapchain texture is available to be
acquired, and then acquires it.
functions.syntax:
bool SDL_WaitAndAcquireGPUSwapchainTexture(
 SDL_GPUCommandBuffer *command_buffer,
 SDL_Window *window,
 SDL_GPUTexture **swapchain_texture,
 Uint32 *swapchain_texture_width,
 Uint32 *swapchain_texture_height);

parameters.name: command_buffer
parameters.description: a command buffer.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: a window that has been claimed.

parameters.name: swapchain_texture
parameters.description: a pointer filled in with a swapchain texture handle.

parameters.name: swapchain_texture_width
parameters.description:
a pointer filled in with the swapchain texture width, may be
NULL.

parameters.name: swapchain_texture_height
parameters.description:
a pointer filled in with the swapchain texture height, may be
NULL.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success, false on error; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks:
When a swapchain texture is acquired on a command buffer, it will
automatically be submitted for presentation when the command buffer is
submitted. The swapchain texture should only be referenced by the
command buffer used to acquire it. It is an error to call SDL_CancelGPUCommandBuffer ()
after a swapchain texture is acquired.

This function can fill the swapchain texture handle with NULL in
certain cases, for example if the window is minimized. This is not an
error. You should always make sure to check whether the pointer is NULL
before actually using it.

The swapchain texture is managed by the implementation and must not
be freed by the user. You MUST NOT call this function from any thread
other than the one that created the window.

The swapchain texture is write-only and cannot be used as a sampler
or for another reading operation.
functions.threadSafety:
This function should only be called from the thread that created the
window.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WaitAsyncIOResult
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Block until an async I/O task queue has a completed task.

parameters.name: queue
parameters.description: the async I/O task queue to wait on.

parameters.name: outcome
parameters.description:
details of a finished task will be written here. May not be
NULL.

parameters.name: timeoutMS
parameters.description:
the maximum time to wait, in milliseconds, or -1 to wait
indefinitely.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true if task has completed, false otherwise.
remarks:
This function puts the calling thread to sleep until there a task
assigned to the queue that has finished.

If a task assigned to the queue has finished, this will return true
and fill in outcome with the details of the task. If no
task in the queue has finished, this function will return false.

If a task has completed, this function will free its resources and
the task pointer will no longer be valid. The task will be removed from
the queue.

It is safe for multiple threads to call this function on the same
queue at once; a completed task will only go to one of the threads.

Note that by the nature of various platforms, more than one waiting
thread may wake to handle a single task, but only one will obtain it, so
 timeoutMS is a maximum wait time, and this
function may return false sooner.

This function may return false if there was a system error, the OS
inadvertently awoke multiple threads, or if SDL_SignalAsyncIOQueue () was
called to wake up all waiting threads without a finished task.

A timeout can be used to specify a maximum wait time, but rather than
polling, it is possible to have a timeout of -1 to wait forever, and use
 SDL_SignalAsyncIOQueue () to
wake up the waiting threads later.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WaitCondition
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Wait until a condition variable is signaled.

parameters.name: cond
parameters.description: the condition variable to wait on.

parameters.name: mutex
parameters.description: the mutex used to coordinate thread access.

remarks:
This function unlocks the specified mutex and waits for
another thread to call SDL_SignalCondition () or SDL_BroadcastCondition () on the
condition variable cond . Once the condition variable is
signaled, the mutex is re-locked and the function returns.

The mutex must be locked before calling this function. Locking the
mutex recursively (more than once) is not supported and leads to
undefined behavior.

This function is the equivalent of calling SDL_WaitConditionTimeout () with
a time length of -1.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WaitConditionTimeout
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Wait until a condition variable is signaled or a certain time has
passed.

parameters.name: cond
parameters.description: the condition variable to wait on.

parameters.name: mutex
parameters.description: the mutex used to coordinate thread access.

parameters.name: timeoutMS
parameters.description:
the maximum time to wait, in milliseconds, or -1 to wait
indefinitely.

functions.returns:
(bool) Returns true if the condition variable is signaled, false if
the condition is not signaled in the allotted time.
remarks:
This function unlocks the specified mutex and waits for
another thread to call SDL_SignalCondition () or SDL_BroadcastCondition () on the
condition variable cond , or for the specified time to
elapse. Once the condition variable is signaled or the time elapsed, the
mutex is re-locked and the function returns.

The mutex must be locked before calling this function. Locking the
mutex recursively (more than once) is not supported and leads to
undefined behavior.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WaitEvent
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Wait indefinitely for the next available event.

parameters.name: event
parameters.description:
the SDL_Event structure to be filled in
with the next event from the queue, or NULL.

functions.returns:
(bool) Returns true on success or false if there was an error while
waiting for events; call SDL_GetError ()
for more information.
remarks:
If event is not NULL, the next event is removed from the
queue and stored in the SDL_Event structure
pointed to by event .

As this function may implicitly call SDL_PumpEvents (), you can only call this
function in the thread that initialized the video subsystem.
functions.threadSafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WaitEventTimeout
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Wait until the specified timeout (in milliseconds) for the next
available event.

parameters.name: event
parameters.description:
the SDL_Event structure to be filled in
with the next event from the queue, or NULL.

parameters.name: timeoutMS
parameters.description:
the maximum number of milliseconds to wait for the next available
event.

functions.returns:
(bool) Returns true if this got an event or false if the timeout
elapsed without any events available.
remarks:
If event is not NULL, the next event is removed from the
queue and stored in the SDL_Event structure
pointed to by event .

As this function may implicitly call SDL_PumpEvents (), you can only call this
function in the thread that initialized the video subsystem.

The timeout is not guaranteed, the actual wait time could be longer
due to system scheduling.
functions.threadSafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WaitForGPUFences
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Blocks the thread until the given fences are signaled.
functions.syntax:
bool SDL_WaitForGPUFences(
 SDL_GPUDevice *device,
 bool wait_all,
 SDL_GPUFence *const *fences,
 Uint32 num_fences);

parameters.name: device
parameters.description: a GPU context.

parameters.name: wait_all
parameters.description:
if 0, wait for any fence to be signaled, if 1, wait for all fences
to be signaled.

parameters.name: fences
parameters.description: an array of fences to wait on.

parameters.name: num_fences
parameters.description: the number of fences in the fences array.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success, false on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WaitForGPUIdle
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Blocks the thread until the GPU is completely idle.

parameters.name: device
parameters.description: a GPU context.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success, false on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WaitForGPUSwapchain
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Blocks the thread until a swapchain texture is available to be
acquired.

parameters.name: device
parameters.description: a GPU context.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: a window that has been claimed.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success, false on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
functions.threadSafety:
This function should only be called from the thread that created the
window.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WaitProcess
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Wait for a process to finish.

parameters.name: process
parameters.description: The process to wait for.

parameters.name: block
parameters.description:
If true, block until the process finishes; otherwise, report on the
process' status.

parameters.name: exitcode
parameters.description:
a pointer filled in with the process exit code if the process has
exited, may be NULL.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true if the process exited, false otherwise.
remarks: This can be called multiple times to get the status of a process.

The exit code will be the exit code of the process if it terminates
normally, a negative signal if it terminated due to a signal, or -255
otherwise. It will not be changed if the process is still running.

If you create a process with standard output piped to the application
( pipe_stdio being true) then you should read all of the
process output before calling SDL_WaitProcess (). If you don't do this
the process might be blocked indefinitely waiting for output to be read
and SDL_WaitProcess () will never
return true;
functions.threadSafety: This function is not thread safe.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WaitSemaphore
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Wait until a semaphore has a positive value and then decrements
it.

parameters.name: sem
parameters.description: the semaphore wait on.

remarks:
This function suspends the calling thread until the semaphore pointed
to by sem has a positive value, and then atomically
decrement the semaphore value.

This function is the equivalent of calling SDL_WaitSemaphoreTimeout () with
a time length of -1.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WaitSemaphoreTimeout
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Wait until a semaphore has a positive value and then decrements
it.

parameters.name: sem
parameters.description: the semaphore to wait on.

parameters.name: timeoutMS
parameters.description:
the length of the timeout, in milliseconds, or -1 to wait
indefinitely.

functions.returns:
(bool) Returns true if the wait succeeds or false if the wait times
out.
remarks:
This function suspends the calling thread until either the semaphore
pointed to by sem has a positive value or the specified
time has elapsed. If the call is successful it will atomically decrement
the semaphore value.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WaitThread
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Wait for a thread to finish.

parameters.name: thread
parameters.description:
the SDL_Thread pointer that was
returned from the SDL_CreateThread ()
call that started this thread.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
a pointer filled in with the value returned from the thread function
by its 'return', or -1 if the thread has been detached or isn't valid,
may be NULL.

remarks:
Threads that haven't been detached will remain until this function
cleans them up. Not doing so is a resource leak.

Once a thread has been cleaned up through this function, the SDL_Thread that references it becomes invalid
and should not be referenced again. As such, only one thread may call SDL_WaitThread () on another.

The return code from the thread function is placed in the area
pointed to by status , if status is not
NULL.

You may not wait on a thread that has been used in a call to SDL_DetachThread (). Use either that
function or this one, but not both, or behavior is undefined.
It is safe to pass a NULL thread to this function; it is a no-op.

Note that the thread pointer is freed by this function and is not
valid afterward.
functions.threadSafety:
It is safe to call this function from any thread, but only a single
thread can wait any specific thread to finish.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WarpMouseGlobal
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Move the mouse to the given position in global screen space.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: the x coordinate.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: the y coordinate.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks: This function generates a mouse motion event.

A failure of this function usually means that it is unsupported by a
platform.

Note that this function will appear to succeed, but not actually move
the mouse when used over Microsoft Remote Desktop.
functions.threadSafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WarpMouseInWindow
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Move the mouse cursor to the given position within the window.
functions.syntax:
void SDL_WarpMouseInWindow(SDL_Window *window,
 float x, float y);

parameters.name: window
parameters.description:
the window to move the mouse into, or NULL for the current mouse
focus.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: the x coordinate within the window.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: the y coordinate within the window.

remarks:
This function generates a mouse motion event if relative mode is not
enabled. If relative mode is enabled, you can force mouse events for the
warp by setting the SDL_HINT_MOUSE_RELATIVE_WARP_MOTION
hint.

Note that this function will appear to succeed, but not actually move
the mouse when used over Microsoft Remote Desktop.
functions.threadSafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WasInit
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Get a mask of the specified subsystems which are currently
initialized.

parameters.name: flags
parameters.description:
any of the flags used by SDL_Init (); see
 SDL_Init for details.

functions.returns:
( SDL_InitFlags ) Returns a mask of
all initialized subsystems if flags is 0, otherwise it
returns the initialization status of the specified subsystems.
functions.threadSafety: This function is not thread safe.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_wcscasecmp
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Compare two null-terminated wide strings, case-insensitively.

parameters.name: str1
parameters.description: the first string to compare. NULL is not permitted!

parameters.name: str2
parameters.description: the second string to compare. NULL is not permitted!

functions.returns:
(int) Returns less than zero if str1 is "less than" str2, greater
than zero if str1 is "greater than" str2, and zero if the strings match
exactly.
remarks:
This will work with Unicode strings, using a technique called
"case-folding" to handle the vast majority of case-sensitive human
languages regardless of system locale. It can deal with expanding
values: a German Eszett character can compare against two ASCII 's'
chars and be considered a match, for example. A notable exception: it
does not handle the Turkish 'i' character; human language is
complicated!

Depending on your platform, "wchar_t" might be 2 bytes, and expected
to be UTF-16 encoded (like Windows), or 4 bytes in UTF-32 format. Since
this handles Unicode, it expects the string to be well-formed and not a
null-terminated string of arbitrary bytes. Characters that are not valid
UTF-16 (or UTF-32) are treated as Unicode character U+FFFD (REPLACEMENT
CHARACTER), which is to say two strings of random bits may turn out to
match if they convert to the same amount of replacement characters.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_wcscmp
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Compare two null-terminated wide strings.

parameters.name: str1
parameters.description: the first string to compare. NULL is not permitted!

parameters.name: str2
parameters.description: the second string to compare. NULL is not permitted!

functions.returns:
(int) Returns less than zero if str1 is "less than" str2, greater
than zero if str1 is "greater than" str2, and zero if the strings match
exactly.
remarks:
This only compares wchar_t values until it hits a null-terminating
character; it does not care if the string is well-formed UTF-16 (or
UTF-32, depending on your platform's wchar_t size), or uses valid
Unicode values.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_wcsdup
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Allocate a copy of a wide string.

parameters.name: wstr
parameters.description: the string to copy.

functions.returns: (wchar_t *) Returns a pointer to the newly-allocated wide string.
remarks:
This allocates enough space for a null-terminated copy of
 wstr , using SDL_malloc , and
then makes a copy of the string into this space.

The returned string is owned by the caller, and should be passed to
 SDL_free when no longer needed.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_wcslcat
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Concatenate wide strings.
functions.syntax: size_t SDL_wcslcat(wchar_t *dst, const wchar_t *src, size_t maxlen);

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description:
The destination buffer already containing the first null-terminated
wide string. Must not be NULL and must not overlap with
 src .

parameters.name: src
parameters.description:
The second null-terminated wide string. Must not be NULL, and must
not overlap with dst .

parameters.name: maxlen
parameters.description: The length (in wide characters) of the destination buffer.

functions.returns:
(size_t) Returns the length (in wide characters, excluding the null
terminator) of the string in dst plus the length of
 src .
remarks:
This function appends up to maxlen - SDL_wcslen (dst) - 1 wide characters from
 src to the end of the wide string in dst , then
appends a null terminator.
src and dst must not overlap.

If maxlen - SDL_wcslen (dst) - 1 is less than or equal to
0, then dst is unmodified.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_wcslcpy
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Copy a wide string.
functions.syntax: size_t SDL_wcslcpy(wchar_t *dst, const wchar_t *src, size_t maxlen);

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description:
The destination buffer. Must not be NULL, and must not overlap with
 src .

parameters.name: src
parameters.description:
The null-terminated wide string to copy. Must not be NULL, and must
not overlap with dst .

parameters.name: maxlen
parameters.description: The length (in wide characters) of the destination buffer.

functions.returns:
(size_t) Returns the length (in wide characters, excluding the null
terminator) of src .
remarks:
This function copies maxlen - 1 wide characters from
 src to dst , then appends a null
terminator.
src and dst must not overlap.

If maxlen is 0, no wide characters are copied and no
null terminator is written.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_wcslen
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
This works exactly like wcslen() but doesn't require access to a C
runtime.

parameters.name: wstr
parameters.description: The null-terminated wide string to read. Must not be NULL.

functions.returns:
(size_t) Returns the length (in wchar_t values, excluding the null
terminator) of wstr .
remarks:
Counts the number of wchar_t values in wstr , excluding
the null terminator.

Like SDL_strlen only counts bytes and
not codepoints in a UTF-8 string, this counts wchar_t values in a
string, even if the string's encoding is of variable width, like
UTF-16.

Also be aware that wchar_t is different sizes on different platforms
(4 bytes on Linux, 2 on Windows, etc).
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_wcsncasecmp
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Compare two wide strings, case-insensitively, up to a number of
wchar_t.
functions.syntax: int SDL_wcsncasecmp(const wchar_t *str1, const wchar_t *str2, size_t maxlen);

parameters.name: str1
parameters.description: the first string to compare. NULL is not permitted!

parameters.name: str2
parameters.description: the second string to compare. NULL is not permitted!

parameters.name: maxlen
parameters.description: the maximum number of wchar_t values to compare.

functions.returns:
(int) Returns less than zero if str1 is "less than" str2, greater
than zero if str1 is "greater than" str2, and zero if the strings match
exactly.
remarks:
This will work with Unicode strings, using a technique called
"case-folding" to handle the vast majority of case-sensitive human
languages regardless of system locale. It can deal with expanding
values: a German Eszett character can compare against two ASCII 's'
chars and be considered a match, for example. A notable exception: it
does not handle the Turkish 'i' character; human language is
complicated!

Depending on your platform, "wchar_t" might be 2 bytes, and expected
to be UTF-16 encoded (like Windows), or 4 bytes in UTF-32 format. Since
this handles Unicode, it expects the string to be well-formed and not a
null-terminated string of arbitrary bytes. Characters that are not valid
UTF-16 (or UTF-32) are treated as Unicode character U+FFFD (REPLACEMENT
CHARACTER), which is to say two strings of random bits may turn out to
match if they convert to the same amount of replacement characters.

Note that while this function might deal with variable-sized
characters, maxlen specifies a wchar limit! If the
limit lands in the middle of a multi-byte UTF-16 sequence, it may
convert a portion of the final character to one or more Unicode
character U+FFFD (REPLACEMENT CHARACTER) so as not to overflow a
buffer.

maxlen specifies a maximum number of wchar_t values to
compare; if the strings match to this number of wchar_t (or both have
matched to a null-terminator character before this number of bytes),
they will be considered equal.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_wcsncmp
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Compare two wide strings up to a number of wchar_t values.
functions.syntax: int SDL_wcsncmp(const wchar_t *str1, const wchar_t *str2, size_t maxlen);

parameters.name: str1
parameters.description: the first string to compare. NULL is not permitted!

parameters.name: str2
parameters.description: the second string to compare. NULL is not permitted!

parameters.name: maxlen
parameters.description: the maximum number of wchar_t to compare.

functions.returns:
(int) Returns less than zero if str1 is "less than" str2, greater
than zero if str1 is "greater than" str2, and zero if the strings match
exactly.
remarks:
This only compares wchar_t values; it does not care if the string is
well-formed UTF-16 (or UTF-32, depending on your platform's wchar_t
size), or uses valid Unicode values.

Note that while this function is intended to be used with UTF-16 (or
UTF-32, depending on your platform's definition of wchar_t), it is
comparing raw wchar_t values and not Unicode codepoints:
 maxlen specifies a wchar_t limit! If the limit lands in the
middle of a multi-wchar UTF-16 sequence, it will only compare a portion
of the final character.

maxlen specifies a maximum number of wchar_t to compare;
if the strings match to this number of wide chars (or both have matched
to a null-terminator character before this count), they will be
considered equal.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_wcsnlen
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
This works exactly like wcsnlen() but doesn't require access to a C
runtime.

parameters.name: wstr
parameters.description: The null-terminated wide string to read. Must not be NULL.

parameters.name: maxlen
parameters.description: The maximum amount of wide characters to count.

functions.returns:
(size_t) Returns the length (in wide characters, excluding the null
terminator) of wstr but never more than
 maxlen .
remarks:
Counts up to a maximum of maxlen wchar_t values in
 wstr , excluding the null terminator.

Like SDL_strnlen only counts bytes and
not codepoints in a UTF-8 string, this counts wchar_t values in a
string, even if the string's encoding is of variable width, like
UTF-16.

Also be aware that wchar_t is different sizes on different platforms
(4 bytes on Linux, 2 on Windows, etc).

Also, maxlen is a count of wide characters, not
bytes!
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.

functions.name: SDL_wcsnstr
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Search a wide string, up to n wide chars, for the first instance of a
specific substring.
functions.syntax: wchar_t * SDL_wcsnstr(const wchar_t *haystack, const wchar_t *needle, size_t maxlen);

parameters.name: haystack
parameters.description: the wide string to search. Must not be NULL.

parameters.name: needle
parameters.description: the wide string to search for. Must not be NULL.

parameters.name: maxlen
parameters.description:
the maximum number of wide characters to search in
 haystack .

functions.returns:
(wchar_t *) Returns a pointer to the first instance of
 needle in the string, or NULL if not found.
remarks:
The search ends once it finds the requested substring, or a null
terminator value to end the string, or maxlen wide
character have been examined. It is possible to use this function on a
wide string without a null terminator.

Note that this looks for strings of wide characters , not
 codepoints , so it's legal to search for malformed and
incomplete UTF-16 sequences.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_wcstol
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Parse a long from a wide string.
functions.syntax: long SDL_wcstol(const wchar_t *str, wchar_t **endp, int base);

parameters.name: str
parameters.description: The null-terminated wide string to read. Must not be NULL.

parameters.name: endp
parameters.description:
If not NULL, the address of the first invalid wide character (i.e.
the next character after the parsed number) will be written to this
pointer.

parameters.name: base
parameters.description:
The base of the integer to read. Supported values are 0 and 2 to 36
inclusive. If 0, the base will be inferred from the number's prefix (0x
for hexadecimal, 0 for octal, decimal otherwise).

functions.returns:
(long) Returns the parsed long , or 0 if no number could
be parsed.
remarks:
If str starts with whitespace, then those whitespace
characters are skipped before attempting to parse the number.

If the parsed number does not fit inside a long , the
result is clamped to the minimum and maximum representable
 long values.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WriteAsyncIO
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Start an async write.
functions.syntax: bool SDL_WriteAsyncIO(SDL_AsyncIO *asyncio, void *ptr, Uint64 offset, Uint64 size, SDL_AsyncIOQueue *queue, void *userdata);

parameters.name: asyncio
parameters.description:
a pointer to an SDL_AsyncIO
structure.

parameters.name: ptr
parameters.description: a pointer to a buffer to write data from.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: the position to start writing to the data source.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: the number of bytes to write to the data source.

parameters.name: queue
parameters.description:
a queue to add the new SDL_AsyncIO
to.

parameters.name: userdata
parameters.description:
an app-defined pointer that will be provided with the task
results.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks:
This function writes size bytes from offset
position in the data source to the area pointed at by
 ptr .

This function returns as quickly as possible; it does not wait for
the write to complete. On a successful return, this work will continue
in the background. If the work begins, even failure is asynchronous: a
failing return value from this function only means the work couldn't
start at all.

ptr must remain available until the work is done, and
may be accessed by the system at any time until then. Do not allocate it
on the stack, as this might take longer than the life of the calling
function to complete!

An SDL_AsyncIOQueue must be
specified. The newly-created task will be added to it when it completes
its work.
functions.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WriteIO
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description:
Write to an SDL_IOStream data
stream.
functions.syntax: size_t SDL_WriteIO(SDL_IOStream *context, const void *ptr, size_t size);

parameters.name: context
parameters.description:
a pointer to an SDL_IOStream
structure.

parameters.name: ptr
parameters.description: a pointer to a buffer containing data to write.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: the number of bytes to write.

functions.returns:
(size_t) Returns the number of bytes written, which will be less than
 size on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks:
This function writes exactly size bytes from the area
pointed at by ptr to the stream. If this fails for any
reason, it'll return less than size to demonstrate how far
the write progressed. On success, it returns size .

On error, this function still attempts to write as much as possible,
so it might return a positive value less than the requested write
size.

The caller can use SDL_GetIOStatus () to determine if the
problem is recoverable, such as a non-blocking write that can simply be
retried later, or a fatal error.

A request for zero bytes on a valid stream will return zero
immediately without accessing the stream, so the stream status (EOF,
err, etc) will not change.
functions.threadSafety:
Do not use the same SDL_IOStream from
two threads at once.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.

functions.name: SDL_WriteSurfacePixel
functions.packageDescription: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3 وتشمل إدارة النافذة والإدخال والأحداث والصوت والربط مع واجهات الرسوميات مثل Vulkan.
functions.description: Writes a single pixel to a surface.
functions.syntax: bool SDL_WriteSurfacePixel(SDL_Surface *surface, int x, int y, Uint8 r, Uint8 g, Uint8 b, Uint8 a);

parameters.name: surface
parameters.description: the surface to write.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: the horizontal coordinate, 0 <= x < width.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: the vertical coordinate, 0 <= y < height.

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: the red channel value, 0-255.

parameters.name: g
parameters.description: the green channel value, 0-255.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: the blue channel value, 0-255.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: the alpha channel value, 0-255.

functions.returns: (bool) Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
remarks:
This function prioritizes correctness over speed: it is suitable for
unit tests, but is not intended for use in a game engine.

Like SDL_MapRGBA , this uses the entire
0..255 range when converting color components from pixel formats with
less than 8 bits per RGB component.
functions.threadSafety:
This function can be called on different threads with different
surfaces.
functions.version: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0.
